# Questions in Livestream 45

Live Stream #45: Amulets and Rumors of Amulets.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=320" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:20</a> | What is the rapture? | He argues for a rapture understood as summary judgment rather than a pre-tribulational rescue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=644" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:44</a> | If humanity establishes space colonies, would those settlements count as part of the `adamah`? | He says the mandate to subdue creation is not obviously limited to Earth, though practical space settlement remains speculative. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=840" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:00</a> | How would you show that biblical cosmology and "pleasing aroma" language are analogical rather than literal? | He appeals to obviously figurative texts, saying Scripture often uses analogy and imagery rather than flat literalism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=1260" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:00</a> | Does documented history show Nebuchadnezzar fulfilled Ezekiel 26:7-11, with Alexander fitting later verses? | He says yes: Nebuchadnezzar fits the initial fulfillment, while the chapter also allows repeated conquests such as Alexander's. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=1641" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:21</a> | Do Ezekiel 26:14, 26:2, and 26:21 mean Tyre would never be rebuilt, or never rebuilt the same way? | He says the point is that Tyre would never return as the same regional power, not that no rebuilding would ever happen. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=1847" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:47</a> | What do the 70 years concerning Tyre mean in Isaiah 23:15-18, and are they connected to Ezekiel 26? | He connects them to exile and later return under Babylonian policy, with overlap in theme but not as a simple one-text explanation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=2154" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:54</a> | In Luke 19:44, was Jesus speaking hyperbolically about Jerusalem's destruction? | He says the saying points to the Roman destruction of Jerusalem, with some hyperbolic force even though the devastation was real. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=2375" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:35</a> | Are Solomon's 700 wives and 300 concubines symbolic or literal? | He treats them as rounded figures expressing a very large royal harem tied to treaties, trade, and political power. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=2722" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:22</a> | What are the top five most important sites related to biblical archaeology? | He gives a ranked-style answer centered on major sites such as Qumran and other key locations for biblical history and texts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=3132" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:12</a> | Is Noah named Noah because he inherited the name from an earlier clan progenitor? | He says the evidence is uncertain, but the clan-progenitor model is possible and worth considering. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=3695" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:35</a> | Have you ever considered leading a tour of Egypt? | Yes; a prior tour was planned but canceled for lack of signups, and he would consider trying again if the channel grew enough. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=3981" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:21</a> | Do you have or recommend any sources on the plagues of Egypt? | He says there is a scholarly gap; Kenneth Kitchen is the best stopgap, though he wants a more detailed book on the plagues. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=4201" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:01</a> | Who sold Joseph into slavery, his brothers or the Midianites/Ishmaelites? | Both: the brothers sold him first, then the traders resold him in Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=4505" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:05</a> | How does the Old Testament compare with other ancient historical accounts of a people group? | It spans a long period with shifting cultural contexts; Israelite practice overlaps with Egypt and Canaan but also differs in diet, ritual, and theology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=5098" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:58</a> | Could you explain Moses' genealogy: was Amram son of Kohath or from the clan of Kohath, and where does Jochebed fit? | He reads Exodus 6 as clan language, with Amram and Kohath as clan/progenitor markers and Jochebed as a related branch-family member. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=5494" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:34</a> | Does Horemheb fit the profile of the pharaoh who wanted to kill Moses? | Yes; he sees Horemheb's anti-corruption, law-and-order profile as fitting a pharaoh who would pursue Moses for killing the taskmaster. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=5819" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:59</a> | Was Tanis destroyed by fire, as some videos claim from burned-looking statues? | He is unsure; he saw a destruction layer, but not burned statues, and says the evidence is limited and unpublished. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=6012" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:12</a> | Were Old Testament laws enforced more or less strictly than laws today? | It varied by law: some were cultural norms, some strictly enforced, and some poorly enforced. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=6415" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:55</a> | Was the golden calf meant as an idol to Yahweh, and should Exodus 32:4 read "these are your gods"? | He says the Hebrew points to plural gods; Aaron places a bovine deity beside Yahweh, violating the covenant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=6986" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:26</a> | Why is evidence for David and Solomon difficult to find from the 11th and 10th centuries BC? | Wet conditions destroy perishable evidence; enough remains support historical David and Solomon, though kingdom size remains debated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=7298" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:38</a> | Is it feasible for Solomon to have had so many wives, and are there ancient parallels? | Yes; large royal harems tied to treaty networks have parallels, especially in Egyptian royal practice, though exact numbers are uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=7562" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:02</a> | Do you recommend English translations of Egyptian texts for general readers? | He recommends William Kelly Simpson's *Ancient Egyptian Literature* and is more cautious about Miriam Lichtheim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=7728" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:48</a> | Was King Abijam/Abijah's maternal grandfather Absalom? | Yes, he says the names line up and Abijah is related to David through both paternal and maternal lines. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=8144" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:44</a> | Is there any evidence for Moses? | No direct inscriptional evidence is expected, but he points to indirect evidence from Moses' Egyptian name, social role, protest parallels, and Exodus itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=8515" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:55</a> | Are there good Exodus evidence series or documentaries? | He points to his own series and Inspiring Philosophy, but says most commercial Exodus documentaries are archaeologically poor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=8830" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:10</a> | Have you ever tried buza, the modern Egyptian version of ancient Egyptian beer? | No; he has tried Egyptian beer generally, but not buza specifically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=8966" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:26</a> | Was bilharzia endemic to ancient Egypt and interpreted as male menstruation? | He cannot answer the epidemiology directly, but discusses Nile parasites, unsafe water, and beer as safer hydration. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=9300" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:00</a> | What is the evidence for and against Israelites originating from Canaanite heritage versus an Exodus out of Egypt? | He argues the Egyptian vocabulary, customs, route details, Avaris, Hazor, and Merneptah Stele favor an Egyptian origin over peaceful Canaanite emergence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=9755" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:35</a> | How long was Horemheb's reign: 14, 19, or 28 years? | He says current majority thinking favors 14 years, based on wine docket evidence, though some scholars argue longer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=10078" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:58</a> | If early biblical genealogies are clans rather than individual ages, how should Genesis 6:3 be read? | He reads the 120 years as the countdown to the flood and the limit of God's patience, not a maximum human lifespan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=10507" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:07</a> | What do you think about *Moon Knight* and its use of Egyptian mythology and history? | He has not seen it yet, but says popular media can generate interest in Egyptology if it is well done. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=10640" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:20</a> | Have you considered using Discord for community interaction outside livestreams? | Yes, but moderation time and resources are the obstacle; he may reconsider if help is available. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=10827" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:27</a> | Are there any good video sources against the documentary hypothesis? | He says books are stronger than videos here, and points listeners toward authors such as Joshua Berman and earlier critics. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=11102" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:02</a> | Is 120 years symbolic, and why did God wait longer to judge Canaan? | The 120 years may be symbolic as a multiple of 40, but the main point is God's limited patience; judgment varies by sin, context, covenant responsibility, and divine patience. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=11850" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:30</a> | What does archaeology tell us about Sodom and Gomorrah? Did they exist? | He thinks they did exist, but says they have not been securely identified archaeologically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/K-auTwN2ygQ?t=12135" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22:15</a> | Did Ramesses II have pets, and what kinds of pets did ancient Egyptians keep? | Yes. He mentions royal horses and dogs, common household cats, and a broader Egyptian fondness for trained animals. |
